stories.title: 2026 Kia WNBA Shooting Stars Participants
stories.summary: Four WNBA legends and current stars, including two-time MVP Elena Delle Donne and five-time WNBA champion Rebekkah Brunson, will compete in the Kia WNBA Shooting Stars timed shooting competition. The event will take place on July 24 at 8 p.m. ET at Wintrust Arena in Chicago, broadcast on ESPN and Disney+. The competition is part of the AT&T WNBA All-Star 2026, which will be followed by the 2026 AT&T WNBA All-Star Game on July 25.

stories.title: Matchday | Timbers back at Providence Park to take on FC Dallas
stories.summary: The Portland Timbers return to Providence Park to face FC Dallas on Wednesday night, with kickoff at 7:30pm PT. The match marks their first home game since the close of the FIFA World Cup. Portland is seeking to build on their recent 5-1 win over the Seattle Sounders. Fans can watch the game live on Apple TV.

stories.title: Halo Campaign Evolved Release Date and Early Access Times Revealed
stories.summary: Halo Campaign Evolved launches on July 28, 2026, at 8 AM PT / 11 AM ET. However, Premium Edition owners can start playing five days early, on July 23, 2026, at 8 AM PT / 11 AM ET. The game is available on PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC in various regions. Early access players can experience the reimagined Halo campaign before the full release.

stories.title: Antoine Griezmann set to make Orlando City debut
stories.summary: French forward Antoine Griezmann is set to make his official debut with Orlando City on Wednesday night against the San Jose Earthquakes at PayPal Park. Griezmann joins the Lions after a decade-plus career in Spain's LaLiga, where he is Atlético's all-time leading scorer with 212 goals. The 37-year-old's arrival aims to revamp Orlando's attack, which also welcomed back striker Daryl Dike. Griezmann's debut comes as the team looks to climb into the Audi MLS Cup Playoffs mix.

stories.title: Olympic Mountain Glory
stories.summary: A 55-million-year-old undersea volcanic rock formation in the Olympic Mountains of northwestern Washington state has been revealed in a NASA image. The rock, primarily basalt, originated beneath the ocean's surface and was uplifted to form the mountain range. The Olympic Mountains' rugged terrain is a key feature of Olympic National Park, which also includes the Pacific coastline and surrounding national forest and tribal lands. Further geological study of this region is expected to provide new insights into the Earth's history.

stories.title: ‘The Wild Robot’ VFX Studio STIM Sets Canary Islands Operation, Planning to Create More Than 100 Jobs
stories.summary: French animation and visual effects company STIM is opening a production center in Spain's Canary Islands, creating over 100 jobs. The operation, STIM Tenerife, is set to begin in August and will initially employ 60 people by the end of 2026. Located in the Canary Islands Special Zone, a low-tax regime, the studio will increase STIM's production capacity while maintaining artistic and technical excellence. Recruitment for high-skilled positions is already underway.

stories.title: Andy Burnham could easily ignore the UK’s falling aid commitment. He would be wise not to | Halima Begum
stories.summary: UK's aid budget is set to decline significantly under the new allocation plan, with a £6.5 billion reduction over four years. The plan, published last week, will see the aid budget fall from £13 billion to £6.5 billion by 2028-29. Malawi's allocation will drop from £50.2 million to £5 million by 2028-29, affecting three-quarters of the population without access to electricity. The new prime minister, Andy Burnham, will face the consequences of these cuts when he takes office.

stories.title: Nobody in Europe knows or cares what Kemi Badenoch thinks. That’s a big mistake | Simon Nixon
stories.summary: British Conservative party leader Kemi Badenoch has committed her party to a policy of conflict with the EU, potentially leading to rupture, by suggesting opposition to withdrawing from the European Convention on Human Rights or ditching net zero commitments makes one "not serious" for Conservative candidacy. This stance was taken after she withdrew the party whip from a critic, Gavin Barwell. The move comes as the party struggles in opinion polls, third behind Labour. The UK's relationship with the EU is now at a critical juncture.

stories.title: Houthis claim attack on oil tankers as US launches more strikes on Iran
stories.summary: US launches 12th consecutive night of strikes on Iran, killing two people, as Yemen's Houthis claim to have attacked two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea. The Houthis targeted the ships with drones and missiles for allegedly violating their naval blockade. The attack comes as the US President had threatened to destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant if Iran attacked a ship in the Strait of Hormuz. The US strikes and Houthi attack raise tensions in the region.

stories.title: Hosepipe ban in force for millions of households - is your area affected?
stories.summary: A hosepipe ban is now in effect for over 20 million households in southern and eastern England and parts of Wales, restricting watering gardens, washing vehicles and windows, and filling pools. The ban is due to successive heatwaves straining water supplies. This is the third major water restriction in the UK this year. Residents are advised to conserve water as the UK prepares for drier summers expected with climate change.

stories.title: Amish Shah Wins Democratic Primary in Swing Arizona House District
stories.summary: Dr. Amish Shah, who beat the choice of the party’s House campaign arm, will face Jay Feely, a Republican, in a battleground district that covers parts of Phoenix.

stories.title: Lego’s Donkey Kong arcade machine lets Mario jump endless barrels — Miyamoto is reportedly happy
stories.summary: A Lego Donkey Kong arcade machine has been created, priced at $200, featuring a basic fully functional game with a working joystick and button. The machine allows Mario to jump endless barrels, utilizing the same original barrels first released in 1989 for Lego pirate sets. The set was reportedly well-received by Mario creator Shigeru Miyamoto. The machine's design is a nostalgic tribute to classic arcade games.

stories.title: Diesel Crisis Puts Poor Farmers Around the World at Risk
stories.summary: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has limited the shipment of diesel, depriving farmers of fuel for irrigation pumps and threatening the food supply.

stories.title: U.S. Military Presence in Jordan Makes It Iranian Target
stories.summary: Relying heavily on U.S. aid and protection, Jordan is unlikely to restrict the American military presence there, despite escalating Iranian airstrikes.

stories.title: Japan’s Leader Invokes Her Hero, Shinzo Abe, to Drive Nationalist Agenda
stories.summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is leveraging her ties to Mr. Abe, the slain Japanese leader, as she pushes to reshape defense and the economy.

stories.title: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic, Trump tech adviser says
stories.summary: A White House adviser has accused China's Moonshot AI of stealing capabilities from top US AI models, specifically Anthropic's Fable AI, to develop its K3 model. The alleged theft occurred through a process called distillation, where a weaker AI extracts answers from a stronger one. Moonshot gained access to restricted Nvidia servers to train its models. The US government claims to have evidence of this alleged theft, which could have significant implications for the global AI landscape.

stories.title: Even camels can't cope: Africa's ships of the desert hit by rising temperatures
stories.summary: Rising temperatures in Africa's Afar region are causing extreme heat stress in camels, with herder Ali Umer reporting eight camel calves lost in the past month. The region is one of the hottest and driest places on Earth, where nomads lead camel caravans. Camel herders are struggling to cope with blistered feet, watery eyes, and skin burns. The intense heat is expected to worsen, affecting camel populations and local livelihoods.

stories.title: Where to Find a Commonwealth Games 2026 Livestream in the U.S. to Watch the Event for Free
stories.summary: The 2026 Commonwealth Games will take place in Glasgow, Scotland, from July 23 to August 2, featuring over 7,000 athletes from more than 70 nations. The event will air live and as nightly highlights on channels like 7Plus in Australia and Channel 5 in the UK. North American viewers can watch the Games for free on CBC, Canada's national public broadcaster, using a virtual private network. The Games will be available online from anywhere in the world with a VPN set to a Canadian location.

stories.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The US and Saudi Arabia have signed a landmark nuclear deal, a "peaceful nuclear co-operation agreement," allowing the Gulf kingdom to develop a civilian nuclear program. The deal provides access for American companies in Saudi's nuclear energy program and includes a bilateral safeguards agreement. The agreement marks a first for the US, potentially enabling Saudi Arabia to enrich uranium in the future. This partnership is expected to advance several economic and strategic objectives, including nuclear nonproliferation.

stories.title: Canada's 'powerful' dairy sector is in Trump's trade crosshairs
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has targeted Canada's dairy sector with a 50% tariff on $20bn worth of Canadian goods imported to the US, set to take effect in August, citing the country's supply management system as an "unreasonable" barrier to American farmers. The system controls production quotas, pricing, and imports of dairy, eggs, and poultry. Canadian politicians have indicated a willingness to stand firm on the system, with Quebec Premier Christine Fréchette calling it non-negotiable. The move is likely to escalate trade tensions between the two nations.

stories.title: Hope for thousands of MS patients as 'life-changing' drug now on NHS in England
stories.summary: Thousands of multiple sclerosis patients in England are set to benefit from the NHS's decision to make the life-changing drug fampridine available, which can help people walk by boosting nerve signals. Up to 5,000 people could benefit each year. The drug has already been shown to improve walking speed and distance in around four in 10 users. This could enable patients to live independently and participate in daily activities.

stories.title: Three Airports Plan to Ditch T.S.A. Agents Amid Push for Private Security
stories.summary: The airports have opted in to the agency’s new security model, an agency spokesperson confirmed on Wednesday. A union official said the move could jeopardize public safety.

stories.title: McInnes glad of Rangers backing in appeal against ban
stories.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has received a four-match touchline ban for criticizing a late penalty awarded to Celtic at Motherwell in May. The ban is in addition to an immediate three-game suspension and one game suspended until the end of the season. McInnes is contesting the penalty, saying it was directed at the decision rather than officials. The appeal is expected to be a key issue in the coming weeks.

stories.title: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
stories.summary: Google's parent company Alphabet reported negative free cash flow of $5.9 billion for the first time in at least a decade, due to soaring spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure. The company's AI costs are expected to reach $205 billion this year, up from $190 billion. This significant investment comes as Alphabet's combined quarterly revenue hit $119.8 billion, a 23% increase from last year. The company's stock fell 4% in after-hours trading.

stories.title: Yeon Sang-ho Returns to ‘Parasyte’ Universe With ‘Tamiya,’ Starring Shioli Kutsuna, at Netflix
stories.summary: South Korean filmmaker Yeon Sang-ho is returning to the "Parasyte" universe with a new live-action series, "Parasyte: Tamiya," starring Shioli Kutsuna, set to stream on Netflix in 2027. The series marks another collaboration between Netflix and Yeon, who developed the show and co-wrote the script with Ryu Yong-jae. This is the latest project from Yeon, known for his work on "Hellbound" and "Parasyte: The Grey." The series is expected to premiere next year.

stories.title: Liverpool confident of signing Barcola - Thursday's gossip
stories.summary: Liverpool is confident of signing 23-year-old Paris St-Germain forward Bradley Barcola, who impressed with four goal contributions at the World Cup with France this summer. The move would see Liverpool beat out Arsenal and Bayern Munich for the player's signature. Barcola's World Cup performance has boosted his transfer value. Liverpool's confidence in securing the signing suggests a potential deal is imminent.

stories.title: Tears and sleepless nights as Marshall and Adlington get Scotland flag honour
stories.summary: Scottish athletes Marshall and Adlington have been chosen to carry the Scotland flag at the Commonwealth Games. Marshall, a 59-year-old bowler, and Adlington, a 39-year-old judo gold medalist, will share the honour. The flag will be presented to them on the day of the big show. The two athletes have been keeping the news a secret for weeks, making it a surprise for everyone else.

stories.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
stories.summary: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health select committee report has found that obesity is an engineered outcome, not a failure of willpower, with sugar and refined carbohydrates identified as primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. The report recommends targeting ultra-processed foods to reduce obesity in Britain, with a focus on reducing sugar content. This shift in approach aims to correct decades of flawed food policy. The next step is for supermarkets and manufacturers to be held accountable for reformulating their products.

offbeat.title: 2026 Kia WNBA Shooting Stars Participants
offbeat.summary: Four WNBA legends and current stars, including two-time MVP Elena Delle Donne and five-time WNBA champion Rebekkah Brunson, will compete in the Kia WNBA Shooting Stars timed shooting competition. The event will take place on July 24 at 8 p.m. ET at Wintrust Arena in Chicago, broadcast on ESPN and Disney+. The competition is part of the AT&T WNBA All-Star 2026, which will be followed by the 2026 AT&T WNBA All-Star Game on July 25.

The game is available on PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC in various regions. Early access players can experience the reimagined Halo campaign before the full release.

The 37-year-old's arrival aims to revamp Orlando's attack, which also welcomed back striker Daryl Dike. Griezmann's debut comes as the team looks to climb into the Audi MLS Cup Playoffs mix.
